policy.rule: Action surfacing and early-retirement impact outrank UI polish.

items.title: Current holdings tab with combined, SKB, parents, and sleeve views
items.why: Useful for understanding concentration and account ownership, but lower priority than making ACT_NOW decisions durable.
items.done_when: Cockpit can switch combined/SKB/parents views and show sleeve concentration without confusing ETF look-through with direct holdings.

items.title: Broker PDF intake and trade-diff reconciliation
items.why: The system needs current account positions after uploaded broker PDFs, including trade diffs since the last snapshot.
items.done_when: Uploaded account PDFs produce account-level positions, combined positions, and a clear diff of buys/sells/trims since the prior snapshot.

items.title: ETF look-through and overlapping sleeve exposure
items.why: Sleeve concentration can be misleading when ETFs overlap with single-name AI, nuclear, or crypto exposure.
items.done_when: Dashboard distinguishes direct sleeve weights from effective look-through exposure and labels estimates clearly.

items.title: Harden Gmail-based Fundstrat intake
items.why: Fundstrat is the source spine; full-body emails should feed source calls and top prospects without storing raw publication bodies.
items.done_when: Routine reliably searches, reads full bodies when permitted, redacts bodies, updates source calls/top prospects, and reports snippet-only discovery as not checked.

items.title: Replace Claude cloud routines with Codex automations
items.why: Recurring acquisition, synthesis, and research queue work should run from repo-owned Codex routine definitions.
items.done_when: All daily/off-hours routines have active Codex automations, repo-owned prompts, and clear not-checked behavior.

items.title: Map which state belongs in Notion, GitHub, or both
items.why: Overlapping storage caused analysis to exist without surfacing. Ownership rules reduce drift.
items.done_when: Each major object type has a source-of-truth owner, mirror policy, and dashboard surfacing path.

items.title: Relabel From-Research priority so it is not confused with conviction
items.why: The From-Research lane maps queue priority into the shared action row confidence field, which can blur research urgency with investment conviction.
items.done_when: The canonical dashboard labels From-Research priority distinctly while keeping action rows and Today's Actions conviction labels clear.

items.title: Use structured ticker fields for Research Queue action promotion
items.why: Off-hours dossiers should promote actionable buy/sell/hold/research items from explicit ticker metadata instead of fragile title parsing.
items.done_when: Research action promotion prefers ticker/symbol fields, preserves process-item filtering, and activates the near-term date clause for plain-title ticker dossiers.

items.title: Validate the Collection-to-Analyst handoff before feed assembly
items.why: Bad source stamps or inconsistent collection metadata can silently contaminate action surfacing before the publish gate ever sees a feed.
items.done_when: Full-build and runtime skeleton paths fail closed on malformed Contract-B snapshots, bad run/source stamps, critical missing sources, and staleness/source-failure drift.

items.title: Accept live connector-shaped Catalyst Calendar rows
items.why: Near-term catalysts should become durable ACT_NOW review items without requiring a manual calendar export shape.
items.done_when: Catalyst intake accepts stdin connector envelopes, flattens Notion-style properties, writes catalysts.json, and preserves full-build ownership of action promotion.

items.title: Extract a shared dashboard action card renderer
items.why: Today's Actions and From Research should not drift apart when action clarity, priority labels, aging, sizing, or goal-impact badges change.
items.done_when: Both lanes render through one ActionCard component while preserving lane-specific footer text, From Research priority labeling, and Today's Actions aging/sizing chips.

items.title: Surface Signal Log rows as watch-only cockpit context
items.why: Morning Scan notes can explain attention and timing, but should not silently create buy/sell actions without a sharper source.
items.done_when: Optional signal_log convention files feed a separately classified dashboard lane, validate row shape, report not-checked when absent, and never promote directly into Today's Actions.

items.title: Keep generated HTML from implying all-clear when lanes are dark
items.why: The static GitHub Pages summary can be seen before the canonical cockpit, so empty actions plus not-checked lanes must not look like a clean day.
items.done_when: Generated HTML labels itself as a summary/export view, surfaces lane-status and feedback context, and shows a caveat when Today's Actions is empty with dark or stale lanes.
